summary: Matched a small fighter audio/SFX portion of src/melee/ft/ft_0881.c, raising the file report to 18.51% matched. The substantive C work implements ft_80088328 and ft_80088478, both handling fighter sound-effect IDs, special-casing IDs derived from sfx_id - 0x80000, using ft_80087D0C remapping, and playing audio through lbAudioAx_80023870 into Fighter fields x2148 and x214C. The PR also converts many old NOT_IMPLEMENTED bodies in the same file to /// #symbol asm-placeholder form and updates config/GALE01/scratches.txt entries around 0x800860C4-0x80088478 from percentage statuses to OK.
what_changed: Removed the unused include of pl/pl_0371.h from ft_0881.c.
Implemented ft_80088328(Fighter* fp, int sfx_id, int sfx_vol, int sfx_pan), replacing a NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub.
Implemented ft_80088478(Fighter* fp, int sfx_id, int sfx_vol, int sfx_pan), replacing a NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub.
Renamed generic arguments in the new implementations to audio-meaningful names: sfx_id, sfx_vol, and sfx_pan.

ft_80088328 now gates behavior on fp->x2225_b6, special-cases FTKIND_GAMEWATCH and FTKIND_SAMUS in the alternate path, filters special SFX IDs via sfx_id - 0x80000 comparisons against 0x3D60U and 0x3D61U, calls ft_80087D0C, ft_80088770, and ft_800887CC, and stores the lbAudioAx_80023870 handle in fp->x2148.

ft_80088478 now filters the same 0x3D60U/0x3D61U SFX IDs, remaps normal IDs through ft_80087D0C, plays them with lbAudioAx_80023870, stores the handle in fp->x214C, and calls ft_80088828 for the 0x3D61U case.

Converted a sequence of remaining unimplemented functions, including ft_80088510, ft_800885A8, ft_80088640, ftCo_800886D8, ft_80088770, ft_800887CC, ft_80088828, ft_80088884, ft_800888E0, ft_8008893C, ft_80088998, ft_80088A50, ft_80088C5C, ft_80089118, and ft_80089228, from NOT_IMPLEMENTED bodies to /// #symbol declarations.
Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt so many scratches in the 0x800860C4 through 0x80088478 range are marked OK instead of explicit percentage scores such as 99.44%, 100%, 96.18%, 90.45%, and similar.
smart_moves: Used domain-specific parameter names for the newly matched functions, making the SFX semantics clear without relying on guessed function names.
Preserved exact unsigned compare behavior by computing temp_ID = sfx_id - 0x80000 and comparing against 0x3D60U and 0x3D61U.

Kept the matching-oriented no-op expression !sfx_id; in ft_80088328, which is likely present only to influence codegen; the diff gives no explanatory comment, so future users should treat it as a matching artifact rather than logic.
Handled the fp->x2225_b6 branch with a narrow fighter-kind switch for FTKIND_GAMEWATCH and FTKIND_SAMUS, avoiding overgeneralizing behavior to every fighter.
Updated scratch status metadata in the same PR as the C matching work, keeping config/GALE01/scratches.txt aligned with functions that are now accepted as OK.
decomp_lessons: For fighter SFX helper functions, channel or voice IDs may be derived from player_id, x221F_b4, and a constant offset; this PR shows lbAudioAx_80023870 called with (fp->player_id * 2) + 42 + fp->x221F_b4 for fp->x2148 and 0x36 + (fp->player_id * 2) + fp->x221F_b4 for fp->x214C.
When matching branches that test encoded SFX IDs, preserve the subtract-then-compare pattern and unsigned constants if that is what the compiler emitted.
Do not assume all 100% decomp.me scratches should remain percentage-labeled in scratches.txt; this PR mass-converts accepted entries to OK while retaining author/id/parent metadata.
Replacing NOT_IMPLEMENTED blocks with /// #symbol placeholders is an accepted way in this codebase to keep remaining assembly-backed functions declared while implementing nearby functions in C.
A small local implementation can require broad scratch-list cleanup if prior scratch entries for neighboring functions are now considered OK.
naming_conventions: Use ft_800xxxxx for unnamed fighter-common functions when no semantic name is known.
Use ftCo_800xxxxx for common fighter-prefixed functions already carrying the ftCo namespace.
Prefer sfx_id, sfx_vol, and sfx_pan over arg0/arg1/arg2 when the arguments are passed to lbAudioAx_80023870.
Keep fighter struct members as raw known offsets/bitfields such as x2148, x214C, x221F_b4, and x2225_b6 when no higher-level field name is established.
Use /// #function_name for remaining nondecompiled/asm-backed function bodies instead of leaving NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Implemented ft_80088328 and ft_80088478 while leaving neighboring functions as /// # asm placeholders, limiting the C-matching surface to functions that were ready.
Removed an include that was no longer needed after the implementation work, reducing accidental dependencies that might affect compilation.
Used explicit u8 casts for sfx_vol and sfx_pan in ft_80088328's lbAudioAx_80023870 call, matching the expected argument-width behavior in that function.
Preserved a suspicious no-op expression, !sfx_id;, in ft_80088328 as part of the matched output.
Used unsigned literal suffixes in SFX ID comparisons, e.g. 0x3D60U and 0x3D61U.
Used direct switch cases for FTKIND_GAMEWATCH and FTKIND_SAMUS under the fp->x2225_b6 path, with default returning without playing audio.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR dump, so there is no recorded reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that fully match ft_80088328 or ft_80088478 after their reported 89% scores.
Search for uses of x2148 and x214C to identify better names for these fighter audio handle fields.
Search neighboring functions ft_80088770, ft_800887CC, and ft_80088828 for cleanup/stop-SFX behavior.
Compare other fighter SFX helper implementations that call lbAudioAx_80023870 with player_id-derived channel offsets.
Investigate whether the !sfx_id; expression in ft_80088328 was later removed, explained, or replaced by a cleaner matching tactic.
